metadata.title: Washington State Climbing Routes & Hazards Master Database
metadata.sources: Mountain Project, AAC, SummitPost, theCrag, Beckey Guides, Mountaineers Club, USFS, NPS

all_routes.name: Godzilla
all_routes.maintenance_notes: High quality route, consistent erosion from climbers

all_routes.name: Rise and Fall
all_routes.maintenance_notes: Anchor P5 requires inspection; documented loose rock in bolt zone

all_routes.name: Tatoosh
all_routes.maintenance_notes: Requires annual/biannual cleaning; vegetation regrowth within 1-2 years

all_routes.name: Ride the Lightning
all_routes.maintenance_notes: P3 anchor photo-documented with unsafe modification attempt; 70m rope mandatory for descent

all_routes.name: Calling Wolfgang
all_routes.incident_history: October 2010: Kong-Bonatti carbon steel hanger internal corrosion failure; 40-50 ft fall; secondary hanger also failed
all_routes.maintenance_notes: Requires pre-climb bolt inspection; pre-1985 Leeper hangers susceptible to stress corrosion cracking

all_routes.name: Japanese Gardens
all_routes.maintenance_notes: Outstanding climbing; feels harder than rating (Index sandbag reputation)

all_routes.name: West Face Grand Central Tower
all_routes.maintenance_notes: Continues shedding loose rock from 2010 slab collapse

all_routes.name: East Tower Direct
all_routes.maintenance_notes: Highest thermal exposure; avoid peak summer hours

all_routes.name: Sloan Peak East Face
all_routes.maintenance_notes: Full-length rappel requirement; high exposure; post-winter debris cleanup recommended

all_routes.name: Sunshine Wall
all_routes.maintenance_notes: Winter specialty; excellent winter climbing when temperatures right

all_routes.name: Jungle Gym Boulder Field (Sport Routes)
all_routes.maintenance_notes: Popular sport climbing destination; multiple routes

all_routes.name: Gallery
all_routes.maintenance_notes: Moderate sport climbing; loose rock hazard

all_routes.name: Beckey Route (SW Face)
all_routes.hazard_summary: Popular weekend crowds, loose gully approach, standard alpine hazards
all_routes.notes: Classic Grade II alpine; Fred Beckey favorite; intensely popular on weekends; early starts recommended

all_routes.name: Midway
all_routes.protection_quality: Tricky, sparse protection for trad leader
all_routes.notes: Well-documented in multiple guidebooks; locally popular moderate

all_routes.name: Southwest Rib
all_routes.area: South Early Winters Spire, Washington Pass
all_routes.protection_details: Sparse bolts on slabs; natural pro in crack pitches
all_routes.hazard_summary: Runout slabs, mixed protection zones, alpine weather exposure
all_routes.notes: Popular early season (snow/ice descent options); Grade III alpine

all_routes.name: Mount Stuart North Ridge
all_routes.protection_quality: Good to runout on gendarme crux
all_routes.protection_details: Two 5.9 pitches on gendarme with variable protection
all_routes.hazard_summary: High exposure crux pitches, gendarme runout, alpine commitment
all_routes.notes: One of 50 North American Classic Climbs; significant alpine undertaking; 15-17 pitch variation from different start points

all_routes.name: Outer Space
all_routes.notes: Classic Grade IV alpine; Fred Beckey documented route

all_routes.name: Orbit
all_routes.protection_details: Sustained varied climbing; natural protection good
all_routes.hazard_summary: Alpine hazards; less crowded than neighboring routes
all_routes.notes: Less crowded alternative to Outer Space

all_routes.name: Serpentine Ridge
all_routes.protection_details: Mixed natural protection; some sparse sections
all_routes.hazard_summary: Grueling approach, long pitch count, high-pass winter conditions early/late season
all_routes.notes: Classic alpine multi-pitch; significant approach commitment via Colchuck Lake and high passes
all_routes.recent_conditions: Seasonal conditions highly variable; July 2026 reported persistent snow patches

all_routes.name: Backbone Ridge + Fin Direct
all_routes.protection_details: Mixed protection; slightly harder Dragontail option
all_routes.hazard_summary: Alpine commitment, exposure on Fin pitch
all_routes.notes: Slightly harder variant of Dragontail traverses
all_routes.recent_conditions: Alpine conditions July 2026 with early-season snow persistence

all_routes.name: Godzilla
all_routes.hazard_summary: Wetness after rain; standard single-pitch hazards
all_routes.notes: Excellent rock quality; recent ascents documented Jan 2026; popular moderate single-pitch classic

all_routes.name: Senior Citizens in Space
all_routes.hazard_summary: Route seepage after rain; friction climbing nuances
all_routes.notes: Hyperclassic moderate multi-pitch in popular Index area

all_routes.name: Beacon Rock Southeast Corner
all_routes.protection_details: Natural feature protection; varied crack systems
all_routes.hazard_summary: Falcon nesting closures (Feb 1-mid-July SE faces), poison oak on some routes
all_routes.notes: Classic moderate multi-pitch; part of 151+ route Beacon Rock system; year-round climbing

all_routes.name: Pleasure Cruise
all_routes.notes: Popular harder multi-pitch at Beacon Rock

all_routes.name: Ragged Edge
all_routes.hazard_summary: Extreme precipitation area (2-3x Seattle rainfall), long dry times after rain, high bug pressure mid-season
all_routes.notes: Darrington area receives 2-3x Seattle rainfall; requires 2+ days to dry after heavy rain

all_routes.name: True Grit
all_routes.notes: Darrington alpine climbing; new area development documented 2025
all_routes.recent_conditions: Helena Ridge area newly developed by Scott Miller 2025 with bolted sport routes nearby

all_routes.name: Skyline (Dinosaur Tower)
all_routes.protection_details: Cams to 2 inches standard; sandstone quality variable
all_routes.hazard_summary: Seasonal state park access closure dates; southwestern exposure heat in summer
all_routes.notes: Popular state park destination; soft closure dates March 15-Oct 15

all_routes.name: Cro-Magnon
all_routes.notes: Peshastin Pinnacles climbing area

all_routes.name: Caveman
all_routes.protection_details: Sandstone cam climbing; cams to 2 inches
all_routes.notes: Harder Peshastin route

all_routes.name: Iconoclast
all_routes.notes: Grade IV alpine; harder Snow Creek Wall route

all_routes.name: Liberty Crack
all_routes.protection_details: Aid climbing route; protection depends on style (free vs aid)
all_routes.hazard_summary: Aid climbing route; sparse protection zones
all_routes.notes: Multiple pitch count variations cited (aid route); Washington classic

all_routes.name: West Ridge (Mt. Stuart)
all_routes.notes: Shorter alternative via Longs Pass/Ingalls Lake approach

all_routes.name: Mary Jane Dihedral
all_routes.notes: Sustained climbing on classic dihedral

all_routes.name: Breakfast of Champions
all_routes.notes: Popular Index multi-pitch

all_routes.name: Hyperspace
all_routes.hazard_summary: Grade IV alpine sustained technical climbing
all_routes.notes: Harder Snow Creek Wall alpine route

all_routes.name: Pony Keg
all_routes.notes: Vantage/Frenchman Coulee; peak season Oct-Apr (wet Cascades winter alternative)

all_routes.name: Stroken the Chicken
all_routes.notes: Moderate Vantage climbing

all_routes.name: Triple Couloirs
all_routes.notes: Ice/mixed climbing alternative on Dragontail
all_routes.recent_conditions: Highly seasonal; July 2026 persistent snow patches reported

all_routes.name: West Ridge (Prusik Peak)
all_routes.notes: Moderate alpine route in Enchantments system

all_routes.name: Crossing the Threshold
all_routes.notes: Vantage climbing; winter-spring dry season destination

all_hazards.title: Vehicular debris hazard from rim
all_hazards.description: Unstable rocks falling from the rim where vehicle traffic occurs; unique hazard of large falling objects from above climbing walls due to off-road vehicle use
all_hazards.mitigation: Be aware of vehicles above; avoid climbing during peak vehicle traffic times

all_hazards.title: Bolt failure on 5.12 route
all_hazards.description: Documented case where climber leaning on bolt caused it to break, resulting in ~15-foot fall before second bolt also failed
all_hazards.mitigation: Avoid excessive force on bolts; always have backup protection

all_hazards.title: Rock quality variation between routes
all_hazards.description: Unclean routes present loose rock and choss; route quality depends heavily on cleaning effort before anchor placement
all_hazards.mitigation: Pre-climb inspection; clean loose rock before leading

all_hazards.title: Hiker-caused rockfall
all_hazards.description: Hikers and off-road vehicle users above climbing area create unnatural rockfall hazard distinct from natural rock failures
all_hazards.mitigation: Wear helmet; be aware of activity above; avoid prime hiking times

all_hazards.title: Vehicle-accessible proximity creating ongoing rockfall risk
all_hazards.description: Near-vehicular access to top creates ongoing risk of rockfall events from traffic vibrations and disturbance
all_hazards.mitigation: Avoid climbing during peak vehicle access times

all_hazards.title: Slow Children route anchor transition hazard
all_hazards.description: Route has 2 bolts with chains/rings at anchor but no lower-offs; unanchored climbers transitioning to rappel exposed to fatal falls. Fatal incident August 28, 2025 - climber unanchored before weighting new system before untying
all_hazards.mitigation: Always tie into anchor before untying old system; never unanchor before actively weighting new anchor

all_hazards.title: Town Wall transition hazard - fatal incident
all_hazards.description: Fatal incident April 6, 2026 involving mishap during transition from top-rope setup to rappel configuration
all_hazards.mitigation: Meticulous system closure; triple-check all anchor transitions

all_hazards.title: Extreme crowding on weekends
all_hazards.description: Up to 40+ cars in parking lot on sunny summer afternoons; overflow parking a quarter-mile away
all_hazards.mitigation: Climb on weekdays; arrive early; be prepared for crowds and traffic

all_hazards.title: Vehicle break-ins
all_hazards.description: Common car break-ins and petty theft at Index parking areas
all_hazards.mitigation: Don't leave valuables in car; use secure parking

all_hazards.title: Rock trundling from crowds
all_hazards.description: Multiple parties on routes create secondary rockfall hazard from rope drag and climber movement
all_hazards.mitigation: Wear helmet; establish no-overlap climbing zones; use hand signals

all_hazards.title: Approach crowding
all_hazards.description: Heavy foot traffic, passing hazards, bottlenecks at belay areas
all_hazards.mitigation: Plan for movement time; establish clear communication

all_hazards.title: Dinner plate basalt fracturing
all_hazards.description: Rock fractures in distinctive 'dinner plates' creating loose choss everywhere except solid routes; requires significant cleaning
all_hazards.mitigation: Extensive pre-climb cleaning; inspect all holds before weighting; wear helmet

all_hazards.title: Vantage Road closure - unstable rock face
all_hazards.description: Vantage Road SW closed west of Silica Road due to unstable roadside rock face and partial rockslide
all_hazards.mitigation: Use alternative access routes; be aware of road status

all_hazards.title: Columnar basalt instability
all_hazards.description: Top layer of columnar basalt around 80 feet high is subject to flaking and loose block formation
all_hazards.mitigation: Avoid top-out exposure; inspect upper sections carefully

all_hazards.title: Uncleaned routes hazard
all_hazards.description: Many routes have not been cleaned of loose rock; climbers placing new anchors must pre-clean all loose material
all_hazards.mitigation: Always pre-clean route; check bolts for loose fragments around placement

all_hazards.title: M&M Wall direct exposure hazard
all_hazards.description: Area requires helmet use; climbers stand directly under other climbers with significant rockfall exposure
all_hazards.mitigation: Mandatory helmet use; establish climbing zones; use hand signals

all_hazards.title: Route-dependent quality variation
all_hazards.description: Route quality varies dramatically depending on how much effort was invested in cleaning
all_hazards.mitigation: Assess route condition before committing

all_hazards.title: Non-standard rap anchor placement
all_hazards.description: Many climbs have rap anchors placed just short of rim to allow lowering without topping off (addressing design considerations but creating positioning challenges)
all_hazards.mitigation: Understand specific anchor placement; be careful with positioning

all_hazards.title: Metamorphic volcanic rock fracture-prone
all_hazards.description: Generally more fractured than nearby Little Si; loose rock is a problem on less-visited areas
all_hazards.mitigation: Inspect before climbing; avoid less-traveled routes; clean loose rock

all_hazards.title: Fracture-prone metamorphics
all_hazards.description: Rock tends toward fracture patterns making loose rock endemic despite appearing chossy
all_hazards.mitigation: Standard loose rock protocols; careful inspection

all_hazards.title: Unvisited route buildup
all_hazards.description: Routes without regular traffic accumulate loose rock; most routes aren't as chossy as they appear but require pre-climb assessment
all_hazards.mitigation: Pre-climb inspection essential; clean accumulations

all_hazards.title: Seasonal crowds
all_hazards.description: Very crowded on weekends and summer evenings with many climbs getting congested
all_hazards.mitigation: Climb weekdays; arrive early; be prepared for crowds

all_hazards.title: Vandalism and theft
all_hazards.description: Vandalism and car-prowling documented as recurring problems
all_hazards.mitigation: Don't leave valuables in car; secure parking

all_hazards.title: Discover Pass requirement
all_hazards.description: Parking at multiple trailheads requires Discover Pass; enforcement and access changes occur periodically
all_hazards.mitigation: Obtain Discover Pass; check current access status

all_hazards.title: Metamorphic volcanic rock type
all_hazards.description: Rock is volcanic metamorphics; varies from extremely grippy rough textured (Blackstone Wall) to slippery sharp bullet rock (World Wall)
all_hazards.mitigation: Adapt grip technique to rock type; adjust beta accordingly

all_hazards.title: Slippery sharp rock on World Wall
all_hazards.description: Bullet, slippery, sharp rock creates edge hazards and difficult grip management
all_hazards.mitigation: Technical grip management; be prepared for slippery conditions

all_hazards.title: Mostly choss-free reputation
all_hazards.description: Reputation for cleaner rock than many Washington areas, but climbers still need standard safety precautions
all_hazards.mitigation: Standard loose rock precautions still essential

all_hazards.title: World Wall high-level sport climbing hazards
all_hazards.description: Possibly Washington's best high-level sport crag; extreme exposure and technical climbing demands
all_hazards.mitigation: Elite climbing skills required; careful route selection; redundant protection

all_hazards.title: Loose rock on less-visited areas
all_hazards.description: Similar to Exit 38, lesser-trafficked crags accumulate loose rock
all_hazards.mitigation: Explore popular routes first; pre-clean less-visited areas

all_hazards.title: Decomposing sandstone
all_hazards.description: Rock varies from good to decomposing sandstone; described as 'somewhere between soft and crumbly'
all_hazards.mitigation: Extensive pre-climb cleaning; avoid weighting questionable holds

all_hazards.title: Poor protection routes
all_hazards.description: Martial Diagonal offers poor rock quality and inadequate protection; many routes have sporty runouts
all_hazards.mitigation: Route selection critical; avoid run-out routes if uncomfortable

all_hazards.title: Crumbly rock sections
all_hazards.description: Some sections of Nirvana Ridge have crumbly rock with scary runouts
all_hazards.mitigation: Route research essential; avoid if uncomfortable with runouts

all_hazards.title: Trigger Finger formation - historic collapse
all_hazards.description: Famous formation fell over in 1978; established risk of major flake/formation failure
all_hazards.mitigation: Avoid suspected unstable formations; be aware of history

all_hazards.title: Rockslide (winter 2010)
all_hazards.description: Significant rock slide on right side of slab likely affected multiple route conditions
all_hazards.mitigation: Be aware of geological change; route conditions may be altered

all_hazards.title: Soil erosion hazard
all_hazards.description: Sandy soil creates constant erosion concerns; exacerbated by 2003 fire damage
all_hazards.mitigation: Respect closures; avoid newly eroded areas; stay on established paths

all_hazards.title: Runout routes hazard
all_hazards.description: Many routes are bolted but sporty runouts are common between protection points
all_hazards.mitigation: Route selection based on comfort with runouts; solid belay strategy

all_hazards.title: Narrow rock crests with exposed anchoring
all_hazards.description: Many climbs end on narrow rock crests atop pinnacles; exposed anchoring and descent
all_hazards.mitigation: Careful anchor assessment; solid rappel protocol

all_hazards.title: Fire damage (2003)
all_hazards.description: Fire swept through area affecting soil stability and vegetation that stabilized slope
all_hazards.mitigation: Be aware of fire damage legacy; respect closures

all_hazards.title: Belay failure - May 1998
all_hazards.description: Documented case where miscommunication between partners caused belay instability; long tie-in failure caused violent swing and head trauma
all_hazards.mitigation: Clear belay communication; verify all connections before climbing

all_hazards.title: Unstable rock slide approach
all_hazards.description: Approach to Squire Creek barred for many years due to unstable rock slide; bushwhacking required near Squire Creek Pass
all_hazards.mitigation: Be aware of hazard; use caution during approach; have escape routes

all_hazards.title: Forest Service road condition
all_hazards.description: Roads generally maintained but may be unsuitable for low-clearance vehicles or blocked by fallen trees/landslides
all_hazards.mitigation: Check road status; use 4WD vehicle if possible; be prepared for obstacles

all_hazards.title: Avalanche chute exposure approach
all_hazards.description: High brush, non-existent tread crossing avalanche chutes, rock slides, and creek beds
all_hazards.mitigation: Use avalanche safety protocol during winter/spring; check forecasts

all_hazards.title: Heavy brush and downed trees
all_hazards.description: Brush high and tread non-existent in several places; many downed trees difficult to climb over/under with heavy pack
all_hazards.mitigation: Allow extra time for approach; use proper foot technique; be prepared for difficult terrain

all_hazards.title: Poor cell coverage
all_hazards.description: Minimal cell phone reception; emergency help is far away
all_hazards.mitigation: Satellite communicator; detailed route plan; conservative climbing decisions

all_hazards.title: Extreme wetness
all_hazards.description: Area exceptionally rainy for Western Washington; climbs may take 2+ days to dry after rainfall
all_hazards.mitigation: Plan for wet conditions; bring extra layers; be aware of water management

all_hazards.title: Intense sun exposure on white granite
all_hazards.description: White granite intensifies sun; protective clothing and extra water needed on hot days
all_hazards.mitigation: Extra sunscreen; UV-protective clothing; abundant water

all_hazards.title: Seasonal bugs
all_hazards.description: Bug problems mid-season can be significant
all_hazards.mitigation: Insect repellent; protective clothing

all_hazards.title: Long rappel requirements
all_hazards.description: Many pitches require full-length rappels; recommend bringing two ropes (70m ideal)
all_hazards.mitigation: Two 70m ropes essential; thorough rappel planning; anchor inspection

all_hazards.title: Hard route access
all_hazards.description: Climbs generally harder to access with no easy routes in the area
all_hazards.mitigation: Be realistic about skills; warm-up elsewhere; good warm-up routine

all_hazards.title: Trail loose rock hazard
all_hazards.description: Loose rock has caused multiple falls on trail; path very narrow and uneven with jagged rocks
all_hazards.mitigation: Careful foot placement; scrambling skills; helmet during approach

all_hazards.title: Soft choss variation
all_hazards.description: Rock oscillates from absolute mud choss to occasionally fine depending on section
all_hazards.mitigation: Route-specific inspection; avoid unreliable sections

all_hazards.title: Hiker-caused trundling
all_hazards.description: Hikers tend to trundle rocks and water bottles from top, creating secondary rockfall for climbers below
all_hazards.mitigation: Wear helmet; avoid high-traffic times; establish safe climbing zones

all_hazards.title: Extreme exposure
all_hazards.description: Ledge is highly exposed with sheer cliffs; intense crevices and cracks with steep drop-offs (thousand feet to forest floor)
all_hazards.mitigation: Solid anchors; redundant protection; solid belay technique

all_hazards.title: Climber stacking exposure
all_hazards.description: Multiple parties create direct fall-line exposure; standard position avoidance required
all_hazards.mitigation: Establish exclusive climbing zones; clear communication; no stacking

all_hazards.title: Slippery rock when wet/icy
all_hazards.description: Rock becomes treacherously slippery from rain or ice
all_hazards.mitigation: Avoid climbing in wet/icy conditions; check weather

all_hazards.title: High winds
all_hazards.description: Winds range from mild to 20-30 mph at the ledge
all_hazards.mitigation: Check wind forecasts; avoid extreme wind days

all_hazards.title: Significant objective rockfall
all_hazards.description: Significant objective hazards including rockfall generated from hikers above climbing area
all_hazards.mitigation: Helmet mandatory; be aware of hiker traffic above; establish safe zones

all_hazards.title: Hiker traffic rockfall
all_hazards.description: Sightseers gather at west side hiking trail above third tunnel; climbers stir up loose rock dragging ropes through Grassy Ledges
all_hazards.mitigation: Helmet mandatory; minimize rope movement; be aware of hiker positions

all_hazards.title: Close calls documented
all_hazards.description: Numerous instances of close calls due to stone fall from above
all_hazards.mitigation: Helmet mandatory; establish safe zones; communication with hiking trails

all_hazards.title: Poor overall gorge rock
all_hazards.description: Much of the rock in the gorge is poor quality, though Beacon has solid steep lines
all_hazards.mitigation: Route selection critical; inspect before climbing

all_hazards.title: Grassy Ledges loose rock hazard
all_hazards.description: Upper area has loose rocks disturbed by rope drag; overhead rockfall exposure
all_hazards.mitigation: Minimize rope movement; helmet mandatory; rope management critical

all_hazards.title: Poison oak exposure
all_hazards.description: Poison oak common at Beacon Rock including on some routes
all_hazards.mitigation: Learn to identify poison oak; protective clothing; post-climb wash

all_hazards.title: 850-foot basalt monolith
all_hazards.description: Massive single feature with concentrated climbing on steep terrain
all_hazards.mitigation: Solid belay; redundant anchors; careful rappel management

all_hazards.title: Andesite column quality
all_hazards.description: Andesite columns (from ancient Goat Rocks volcano) are generally solid but fair bit of loose stuff on slopes above cliff
all_hazards.mitigation: Inspect upper areas; be aware of loose material hazard

all_hazards.title: Newcomer grade full value
all_hazards.description: Routes are full-value grades; gear can be fiddly
all_hazards.mitigation: Expect routes to feel harder than grade; bring diverse gear

all_hazards.title: Rockfall from above
all_hazards.description: Keep eye out for rockfall when people above cliff; particularly hazardous with instructional groups
all_hazards.mitigation: Helmet use; be aware of people above; establish safe zones

all_hazards.title: Loose material on slopes
all_hazards.description: Fair amount of loose material on slopes above climbing cliffs
all_hazards.mitigation: Be aware of upper exposure; helmet use; minimize activity above

all_hazards.title: Rattlesnakes
all_hazards.description: Rattlesnakes present in lower canyon; alert needed on approach hike and cliff base
all_hazards.mitigation: Make noise on approach; watch for snakes; keep distance

all_hazards.title: Desert heat exposure
all_hazards.description: Dry desert environment allows year-round climbing but exposes climbers to heat stress
all_hazards.mitigation: Abundant water; UV protection; limit midday climbing

all_hazards.title: Raptor nesting closure
all_hazards.description: Royal Columns and the Bend closed Feb 1 - April 1 for raptor nesting protection; may extend to July 1 if active nest nearby
all_hazards.mitigation: Check closure dates; plan trips outside closure periods

all_hazards.title: Steep terrain sections
all_hazards.description: Hazardous terrain with very steep sections present throughout
all_hazards.mitigation: Solid climbing skills; careful route selection

all_hazards.title: Creek exposure
all_hazards.description: Creek roaring through gorge with surprisingly powerful flow; midway bridge crosses powerful water
all_hazards.mitigation: Careful foot placement; be aware of water; solid balance

all_hazards.title: Raptor nesting closure
all_hazards.description: Bridge Creek Wall area closed Jan 1 through mid-August for golden eagle nesting (1/2-mile buffer required)
all_hazards.mitigation: Check closure status; plan trips outside closure periods

all_hazards.title: Threatened plant species
all_hazards.description: Threatened or endangered plant species growing in cracks near crags; establishment of new routes requires ranger consultation
all_hazards.mitigation: Don't establish new routes without consultation; respect existing route restrictions

all_hazards.title: Human-triggered avalanche
all_hazards.description: Human-triggered avalanches expected on steep slopes; climbers should stay in low-angle terrain and check forecast
all_hazards.mitigation: Check avalanche forecast; avoid steep slopes; conservative route choice

all_hazards.title: Private land crossing
all_hazards.description: Private land access issues in parts of Icicle Creek Canyon; climbers must cross private land to reach National Forest crags in some places
all_hazards.mitigation: Obtain landowner permission; use established access routes

all_hazards.title: Private land crags
all_hazards.description: Some crags themselves located on private land creating access tension
all_hazards.mitigation: Check ownership; obtain permission; respect posted restrictions

all_hazards.title: Scary riverside highballs
all_hazards.description: Dozens of scary riverside highballs and water solos; falls potentially fatal
all_hazards.mitigation: Solid bouldering skills; spot quality; conservative route selection

all_hazards.title: Water solo hazards
all_hazards.description: Cold water immersion, impact injuries on boulders, hypothermia risk
all_hazards.mitigation: Solid swimming skills; water temperature assessment; rescue planning

all_hazards.title: Rockfall bowling alley
all_hazards.description: Late-season parties find themselves in a bowling alley of rockfall triggered by climbers above
all_hazards.mitigation: Helmet mandatory; early season preferred; be aware of parties above

all_hazards.title: Colfax Serac zone massive ice collapse
all_hazards.description: Serac zone presents massive overhead ice collapse risk; any party in fall line is in serious trouble
all_hazards.mitigation: Avoid serac zone; early season preferred; avalanche gear; careful routing

all_hazards.title: Climate warming effect
all_hazards.description: Climate warming has made permanent snowfields looser and prompted more rockfall and icefall
all_hazards.mitigation: Early season climbing; be aware of warming trends; adapt routes accordingly

all_hazards.title: Class 3-4 rock exposure
all_hazards.description: Features Class 3-4 rock with intense exposure and real crevasse navigation hazard
all_hazards.mitigation: Alpine climbing skills; careful route finding; solid belay systems

all_hazards.title: Seasonal transformation danger
all_hazards.description: Early summer AI2-3 snow route transforms by August; bergschrund and moat problems can make it impassable
all_hazards.mitigation: Early season climbing essential; check current conditions; avoid late season

all_hazards.title: Descent crux
all_hazards.description: Real crux on many routes is the descent with multiple rappels down jagged ridges and couloirs
all_hazards.mitigation: Plan descent thoroughly; bring 70m ropes; solid rappel skills; early start

all_hazards.title: Rope drag and bivouac risk
all_hazards.description: Rope drag and snags on descent cause regular party bivouacs due to underestimating descent time under fatigue
all_hazards.mitigation: Conservative time estimates; early start; efficient movement; bivouac kit

all_hazards.title: Seasonal bergschrund hazard
all_hazards.description: Seasonal hazards including bergschrund and moat progression problems
all_hazards.mitigation: Early season preferred; check current conditions

all_hazards.title: Glacier recession route changes
all_hazards.description: Glacier recession is rewriting beta for every classic climb; most guidebooks haven't caught up
all_hazards.mitigation: Recent trip reports essential; avoid relying on dated guidebooks

all_hazards.title: Route-specific glacial hazard obsolescence
all_hazards.description: Historical climbing information becoming unreliable as glaciers retreat
all_hazards.mitigation: Get latest conditions; talk to recent climbers; conservative routing

all_hazards.title: High avalanche danger
all_hazards.description: Avalanche danger commonly high due to notoriously variable weather creating unstable snow even on moderate slopes
all_hazards.mitigation: Check avalanche forecast daily; early start; conservative route choice

all_hazards.title: Avalanche and spindrift hazard
all_hazards.description: Avalanches and spindrift regularly course down Shuksan's precipitous slopes
all_hazards.mitigation: Avalanche training; forecast checking; conservative routing

all_hazards.title: Winter storm locking
all_hazards.description: Mountain can be locked in winter storms for weeks at a time
all_hazards.mitigation: Check weather window; be prepared for rapid weather change; conservative timeline

all_hazards.title: Snowfall extremes
all_hazards.description: Region receives 600+ inches annually; depths can exceed 20 feet on upper slopes during intense storms
all_hazards.mitigation: Winter-specific skills; avalanche training; conservative route choice

all_hazards.title: Glacier crevassing
all_hazards.description: Glaciers particularly sensitive to climate changes; severely crevassed as result
all_hazards.mitigation: Crevasse rescue skills; rope team protocol; careful route finding

all_hazards.title: Glacier thermal venting
all_hazards.description: Active volcano with thermal venting causing sudden avalanches and lahars (mud/debris flows)
all_hazards.mitigation: Avoid thermal venting zones; be aware of active volcano; local knowledge critical

all_hazards.title: Notoriously unpredictable weather
all_hazards.description: Notoriously unpredictable weather; clear conditions can become dangerous without warning
all_hazards.mitigation: Check multiple forecasts; be prepared for rapid changes; early descents

all_hazards.title: Limited climbing windows
all_hazards.description: Windows with good climbing conditions and safe avalanche conditions are few and far between
all_hazards.mitigation: Plan for narrow windows; be flexible; good weather intelligence

all_hazards.title: Rappel anchor failure - May 2025
all_hazards.description: Three climbers killed in fall from Early Winters area; presumed single fixed piton pulled while used as rappel anchor; 400-foot fall
all_hazards.mitigation: Never rappel off single piece; redundancy essential; anchor assessment critical

all_hazards.title: Communication failure on anchors
all_hazards.description: Less experienced climber assumed other climber had assessed single piton; no discussion to bring anchor quality into question (May 2025 North Cascades incident)
all_hazards.mitigation: Always discuss anchor assessment; no assumptions; speak up about concerns

all_hazards.title: Expert halo cognitive bias
all_hazards.description: Higher-skilled climbers may not have their anchor assessment questioned by less experienced partners
all_hazards.mitigation: Speak up regardless of skill level; collaborative safety assessment

all_hazards.title: Single-piece rappel practice hazard
all_hazards.description: Rappelling off single piece of gear is very bad practice; redundant protection is essential
all_hazards.mitigation: Always use redundancy; 2+ pieces minimum; verify anchor quality

all_hazards.title: Failing to back up critical protection
all_hazards.description: Failing to provide redundancy for critical single pieces of protection is persistent factor in fatal accidents
all_hazards.mitigation: Build redundancy into all critical points; double-check before committing

all_hazards.title: 1980s-1990s bolt lifespan issue
all_hazards.description: Bolts installed in 1980s-1990s are reaching end of projected 20-year lifespan; hundreds of thousands need replacement
all_hazards.mitigation: Bolt assessment skills; report bad bolts; support bolt replacement efforts

all_hazards.title: Plated steel corrosion
all_hazards.description: Plated steel bolts subject to corrosion; stainless steel or titanium recommended but less commonly used
all_hazards.mitigation: Inspect bolts regularly; prefer stainless steel; report corrosion

all_hazards.title: Stress Corrosion Cracking (SCC)
all_hazards.description: Certain stainless steel grades subject to SCC leading to sudden brittle fracture with little external warning
all_hazards.mitigation: Industry knowledge; proper bolt installation; regular inspection

all_hazards.title: No standard bolt placement training
all_hazards.description: There is no standard practice or training for placing bolts; compounding as more routes developed
all_hazards.mitigation: Seek proper training before placing bolts; follow industry standards

all_hazards.title: UV exposure sling weakness
all_hazards.description: Nylon webbing weakened by UV exposure; sling rated 22 kN new can degrade to 11 kN or below after single season at 3000m+ altitude
all_hazards.mitigation: Replace slings regularly; UV protection; altitude-specific replacement schedule

all_hazards.title: Freeze-thaw cycle degradation
all_hazards.description: Sling material weakened by freeze-thaw cycles and precipitation
all_hazards.mitigation: Regular sling replacement; winter-specific gear checks

all_hazards.title: Anchor chain reliability
all_hazards.description: Anchor chains can fail after years of wear; inspection required
all_hazards.mitigation: Regular anchor inspection; replace worn chains; know inspection signs

all_hazards.title: Lightning exposure on vertical terrain
all_hazards.description: Lightning strikes highest points first; vertical terrain with nowhere to run for cover creates prime target conditions
all_hazards.mitigation: Check weather; descend at first sign of storm; avoid climbing in lightning

all_hazards.title: Heat exhaustion hazard
all_hazards.description: Temperatures 90F+ create dramatic increase in heat-related illness risk; sudden confusion, fatigue, headache, muscle cramps, pale skin, profuse sweating, rapid heartbeat
all_hazards.mitigation: Abundant water; electrolytes; early starts; break in shade; temperature monitoring

all_hazards.title: Hypothermia in summer conditions
all_hazards.description: Hypothermia possible even in summer from wet conditions, wind, and inadequate layers
all_hazards.mitigation: Proper layering; wind protection; dry clothes; emergency protocols

all_hazards.title: Wind exposure at crags
all_hazards.description: Variable wind speeds; exposed terrain can channel dangerous wind gusts
all_hazards.mitigation: Check wind forecasts; avoid extreme wind days; be aware of terrain wind channeling

all_hazards.title: Altitude sickness risk
all_hazards.description: High-altitude illness occurs above 2500m (8202 feet); includes AMS, HAPE, HACE
all_hazards.mitigation: Gradual ascent; acclimatization; hydration; symptom recognition; descent plan

all_hazards.title: Rapid ascent hazard
all_hazards.description: Going too high too fast primary cause of altitude sickness; oxygen molecules spread farther apart at altitude
all_hazards.mitigation: Limit ascent rate to 1000 feet/day above 10000 feet; acclimatization days

all_hazards.title: Inadequate acclimatization time
all_hazards.description: Proper acclimatization takes 1-3 days; recommended ascent rate 1000 feet/day above 10000 feet
all_hazards.mitigation: Plan extra days; respect acclimatization schedule; symptoms awareness

all_hazards.title: Dehydration at altitude
all_hazards.description: Altitude accelerates dehydration while reducing thirst sensation; critical for alpine climbing parties
all_hazards.mitigation: Drink regularly (not by thirst); carry water; electrolyte supplementation

all_hazards.title: Partner miscommunication
all_hazards.description: Critical safety failures result from miscommunication about anchor systems, belay configuration, and equipment status
all_hazards.mitigation: Clear verbal communication; explicit confirmation; no assumptions

all_hazards.title: Belay inattention
all_hazards.description: Lack of attention is leading cause of belay accidents; delayed reaction to climber needs
all_hazards.mitigation: 100% attention on climber; minimize distractions; active belay technique

all_hazards.title: Lowering accidents from miscommunication
all_hazards.description: Nearly 80 lowering incidents/fatalities over past decade; nearly 100% preventable through proper communication and system closure
all_hazards.mitigation: Clear lowering protocols; system closure verification; locked-off device check

all_hazards.title: Rope slip behind leg during fall
all_hazards.description: Rope sliding behind climber's leg causes inversion at protection; flips climber, causing back/head impact
all_hazards.mitigation: Proper harness fit; leg loop snugness; awareness during falls

all_hazards.title: Excessive rope drag
all_hazards.description: Excessive rope drag makes clipping difficult or extends falls; can turn manageable fall into dangerous situation
all_hazards.mitigation: Route planning; quickdraw placement strategy; minimize zig-zag routes

all_hazards.title: Rope system not closed
all_hazards.description: Critical failure where non-load end of rope not tied to belayer/anchor/something; allows rope to slip through belay device
all_hazards.mitigation: Always tie end of rope; explicit end-of-rope protocol

all_hazards.title: Too much slack provision
all_hazards.description: Belay errors including providing excessive slack cause ground falls or hard rock impacts
all_hazards.mitigation: Proper slack management; active belay; good communication

all_hazards.title: Incorrect belay device operation
all_hazards.description: Improper use of belay/rappel devices leads to accidents; overcomplicated safety systems increase failure probability
all_hazards.mitigation: Proper training; practice; use simple reliable systems; drills

all_hazards.title: Improperly tied knots
all_hazards.description: Failure to properly tie critical knots creates single-point failures
all_hazards.mitigation: Proper knot tying; visual verification; backup knots; regular practice

all_hazards.title: Inadequate backup systems
all_hazards.description: Lack of secondary protection redundancy common in fatal accidents
all_hazards.mitigation: Build redundancy; 2+ protection pieces minimum; verify all pieces

all_hazards.title: Harness degradation over time
all_hazards.description: Harness materials degrade; worn harnesses (particularly belay/rappel loops) can fail under load
all_hazards.mitigation: Regular harness inspection; replace at end of life; maintain records

all_hazards.title: Makeshift harness failure
all_hazards.description: Ad-hoc harnesses fashioned from kayak straps/leather belts have failed fatally
all_hazards.mitigation: Use proper climbing harness; no ad-hoc solutions; proper training

all_hazards.title: Chest strap omission hazard
all_hazards.description: Failure to use chest strap creates airway closure risk and inversion hazard under loaded conditions
all_hazards.mitigation: Always use chest strap; proper fit; make it standard practice

all_hazards.title: Worn soft goods degradation
all_hazards.description: Old webbing and slings lose strength over time; single-season degradation documented
all_hazards.mitigation: Regular replacement schedule; inspection; altitude/weather-specific replacements

all_hazards.title: Overestimating climbing ability
all_hazards.description: Climbers regularly overestimate what they're capable of; grade mismatches lead to severe injuries
all_hazards.mitigation: Honest self-assessment; start well below limit; build gradually

all_hazards.title: Regional climbing style unfamiliarity
all_hazards.description: Routes in unfamiliar rock types feel more difficult; 5.7 slab vs 5.7 crack have vastly different difficulty
all_hazards.mitigation: Start conservative; talk to locals; respect regional grade variations

all_hazards.title: Inadequate equipment knowledge
all_hazards.description: Misunderstanding of equipment capabilities and limitations leads to unsafe systems
all_hazards.mitigation: Proper training; read manuals; understand limitations; ask questions

all_hazards.title: Failure to turn back
all_hazards.description: Climbers continue climbing beyond personal ability; particularly dangerous in alpine environments
all_hazards.mitigation: Recognize limits; planning retreat; no ego in turning back

all_hazards.title: Cascade loose rock endemic
all_hazards.description: Cascade range notorious for loose rock; fundamental challenge across multiple crags
all_hazards.mitigation: Expect loose rock; helmet use; cleaning; foot/hand assessment

all_hazards.title: Rotten/decomposing rock
all_hazards.description: Soft, decomposing, or rotten rock dangerous to climb past; decomposes in place (particularly at Peshastin, Darrington)
all_hazards.mitigation: Avoid rotten rock; know where it exists; cleaning before weighting

all_hazards.title: Barely attached holds and flakes
all_hazards.description: Blocks, flakes, and holds barely attached to cliff faces; climber weight can trigger failure
all_hazards.mitigation: Test before weighting; helmet use; lead partner spotting on boulder

all_hazards.title: Grade standardization problems
all_hazards.description: Washington routes often felt as 'full value' grading; regional variation makes out-of-state climber assessment difficult
all_hazards.mitigation: Start conservative; ask locals; adjust expectations for region

all_hazards.description: Intensely popular on weekends; large parties waiting on approach and route
all_hazards.mitigation: Early starts recommended; mid-week climbing preferred

all_hazards.description: Loose gully terrain on approach to climbing area
all_hazards.specific_location: Approach gully west of Washington Pass
all_hazards.mitigation: Stay alert to rockfall from other parties; avoid approach during peak traffic

all_hazards.description: Standard alpine hazards: weather changes, cold exposure, commitment
all_hazards.mitigation: Alpine skills required; weather forecast mandatory

all_hazards.description: Tricky, sparse protection for trad leader; requires experienced rack building
all_hazards.mitigation: Experienced trad leaders only; consider top-rope first ascent

all_hazards.description: Step-across in pitch 2 with exposure; commitment hazard
all_hazards.mitigation: Solid climbing ability; comfortable with exposure

all_hazards.description: Significant runout slab pitches with sparse protection
all_hazards.mitigation: Requires bold alpine trad climbing experience; confident slab technique

all_hazards.description: Route transitions between runout slabs and crack pitches; protection variable
all_hazards.mitigation: Alpine trad skills essential; mixed climbing proficiency

all_hazards.description: Alpine exposure to rapid weather changes; thunderstorm hazard in July-August
all_hazards.mitigation: Early starts; weather forecast mandatory; lightning awareness

all_hazards.description: Alpine rockfall from upper sections and loose rock
all_hazards.mitigation: Helmets mandatory; be aware of other parties above

all_hazards.description: Two 5.9 crux pitches on gendarme with significant exposure and variable protection
all_hazards.mitigation: Competent 5.9 trad climber essential; bomber protection-seeking approach

all_hazards.description: Long alpine commitment; 20+ pitch route requires full day; limited descent options
all_hazards.mitigation: Alpine experience mandatory; physical conditioning; fast climbing pace

all_hazards.description: Alpine climbing at 9470 ft; altitude, weather, cold, exposure
all_hazards.mitigation: Alpine skills; acclimatization; weather awareness

all_hazards.description: Alpine exposure on Grade IV route; commitment hazard
all_hazards.mitigation: Alpine experience; confident on exposed terrain

all_hazards.description: Moderate rockfall hazard in icicle Canyon area
all_hazards.mitigation: Helmets; awareness of other parties; avoid approach midday

all_hazards.description: Alpine exposure on sustained multi-pitch climbing
all_hazards.mitigation: Alpine experience required

all_hazards.description: Grueling approach via Aasgard Pass and high alpine terrain; 4+ hour approach; full day commitment
all_hazards.mitigation: Alpine hiking skills; early start; navigation ability; headlamp backup

all_hazards.description: 14-pitch alpine climbing; long duration route (8-10+ hours summit time); limited descent
all_hazards.mitigation: Alpine experience; fast climbing pace; ropework efficiency

all_hazards.description: Early/late season snow hazard; persistent snowfields early (July 2026 reported); avalanche risk
all_hazards.specific_location: Entire alpine route and approach passes
all_hazards.mitigation: July-August optimal; snow awareness; avalanche knowledge if conditions marginal

all_hazards.description: Fin Direct crux pitch with exposure; harder variant of Dragontail
all_hazards.mitigation: Solid 5.9+ trad climbing; exposure tolerance

all_hazards.description: 14-pitch alpine route; full day commitment similar to Serpentine Ridge
all_hazards.mitigation: Alpine experience; efficient climbing

all_hazards.description: Rock seeps and stays wet 2-5 days after heavy rain in wet Index climate
all_hazards.specific_location: Entire route; vertical seeping sections wet longest
all_hazards.mitigation: Check weather; wait 2-3 days after rain; friction slabby areas dry faster

all_hazards.description: Route seepage in wet climate (2-3x Seattle rainfall); vertical sections dry slowest
all_hazards.mitigation: Plan for wet rock; friction climbing technique; check recent conditions

all_hazards.description: Peregrine falcon nesting closure: SE faces Beacon Rock closed Feb 1-mid-July annually
all_hazards.mitigation: Plan climbing for mid-July onwards; check current closure status; rangers patrol

all_hazards.description: Poison oak present on some Beacon Rock routes at lower elevations
all_hazards.specific_location: Lower elevation portions of some routes
all_hazards.mitigation: Learn to identify; avoid contact; wash after climbing

all_hazards.description: Potential falcon nesting closure depending on route location (SE faces only)
all_hazards.specific_location: Beacon Rock; check specific route exposure
all_hazards.mitigation: Verify route face direction; check current closures

all_hazards.description: Darrington area receives 2-3x Seattle rainfall; routes require 2+ days minimum to dry after heavy rain; some take weeks
all_hazards.mitigation: Plan for extended dry time; check recent weather; avoid climbing after rainfall

all_hazards.description: High insect (mosquito/fly) pressure mid-season June-August in Darrington area
all_hazards.mitigation: Head net; insect repellent; May/September preferred over summer

all_hazards.description: Darrington area extreme wet climate; 2-3x Seattle rainfall; extended dry times
all_hazards.mitigation: Plan for wet conditions; check extended forecasts

all_hazards.description: Peshastin Pinnacles State Park soft closure: March 15-Oct 15 official season
all_hazards.mitigation: Plan climbing within official season; check park rules at kiosk

all_hazards.description: Sandstone climbing area; variable rock quality; some sections prone to erosion
all_hazards.mitigation: Test holds; conservative protection; sandbag the grade

all_hazards.description: Sandstone protection reliability variable; careful cam placement essential
all_hazards.mitigation: Experienced with sandstone protection; bomber placement technique

all_hazards.description: Grade IV alpine; sustained 5.10c climbing; significant alpine commitment
all_hazards.mitigation: Solid alpine trad climbing skills; 5.10c+ ability; speed

all_hazards.description: Alpine exposure; high-altitude climbing hazards
all_hazards.mitigation: Alpine experience required

all_hazards.description: Aid climbing route (5.11a A0/C2); protection depends on climbing style (free vs aid)
all_hazards.mitigation: Aid climbing proficiency required; style-dependent protection assessment

all_hazards.description: Multiple pitches with sparse natural protection; aid climbing hazard
all_hazards.mitigation: Aid climbing experience; conservative gear philosophy

all_hazards.description: Alpine commitment route; 3+ hour approach; full day climbing
all_hazards.mitigation: Alpine experience; early start; stamina

all_hazards.description: Sustained 5.9 dihedral climbing; endurance hazard on alpine route
all_hazards.mitigation: Solid 5.9 endurance; consistent climbing pacing

all_hazards.description: Index wet climate; routes stay wet after rain
all_hazards.mitigation: Check conditions; plan for wet rock

all_hazards.description: Technical 5.11a crack climbing; sustained difficulty; alpine Grade IV commitment
all_hazards.mitigation: Solid 5.11a+ trad crack technique; alpine endurance

all_hazards.description: Vantage climbing optimal Oct-Apr only; outside this window area too hot/wet
all_hazards.mitigation: Plan climbing Oct-Apr; avoid summer/wet season attempts

all_hazards.description: Vantage area winter-spring climbing window only (Oct-Apr peak); hot/wet outside
all_hazards.mitigation: Respect seasonal window; check weather forecasts

all_hazards.description: Alpine ice/mixed climbing; avalanche and crevasse hazards; snow-dependent
all_hazards.mitigation: Alpine ice climbing skills; avalanche training; crevasse rescue proficiency

all_hazards.description: Highly season-dependent; July 2026 showed persistent snowfields to 5300+ ft; route viability varies year-to-year
all_hazards.mitigation: July-August optimal; check current conditions; very early/late season may not be climbable

all_hazards.description: Alpine exposure; high-pass commitment; 8+ hour summit time
all_hazards.mitigation: Alpine experience; efficient climbing

all_hazards.description: Winter-spring climbing window (Oct-Apr); outside this window hot/wet conditions
all_hazards.mitigation: Restrict climbing to Oct-Apr season
